evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` keeps two access paths (`getSupabaseClient()` plus deprecated exported `supabase` proxy) and lazy-fallback initialization, which preserves legacy behavior but blurs the intended runtime boundary.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` still exports deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) while acting as a cross-cutting facade used broadly, concentrating ownership in a shared singleton-style entrypoint.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` is a deprecated compatibility barrel that re-exports from multiple mobile modules, extending migration overlap instead of enforcing one import surface.

Event-driven orchestration is spread across feature hooks (`src/app/hooks/useSettingsModal.ts` listening for `openSettings`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` listening for `selectShotForAddition`), creating implicit cross-feature coupling.

`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` re-exports `TouchableTooltip` from `touchableTooltip.tsx`, while `touchableTooltip.tsx` imports `Tooltip*` symbols back from `./tooltip`, creating a bidirectional module seam.

Task/orchestrator boundary contracts are duplicated across multiple payload shapes and query predicates: `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` writes nested `orchestrator_details`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` writes mixed top-level and nested orchestrator identifiers, and `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` must query several JSON paths (`params->>orchestrator_run_id`, `params->>run_id`, `params->orchestrator_details->>run_id`, etc.).

`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` depends on many alternate routing fields (`parent_generation_id` at top-level and in `orchestrator_details`, `child_order`/`segment_index`/`join_index`) which indicates integration seams are permissive instead of explicit.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` defines a very large `UseImageEditValueParams` bag and composes multiple partial builders, increasing call-site ceremony and making local behavior hard to track.

`useImageEditValue` memoizes on `[params]`, but `params` is an object assembled by `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`; this undermines the intended memo granularity because identity changes can invalidate the memo wholesale.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts` is a pass-through remapping layer that copies nested objects field-by-field without changing semantics, adding indirection and maintenance surface.

Many assigned files are pure compatibility wrappers with no added behavior (for example `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts`, `src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts`, `src/shared/lib/clientThumbnailGenerator.ts`).

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` and `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` both keep deprecated exports with dated removal targets, but there is no automated enforcement path in these files beyond comments.

The migration surface is still broad in high-traffic entrypoints (`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`), indicating ongoing dual-path maintenance.
`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` types `taskData` as `unknown` but immediately accesses `taskData.params`, `taskData.category`, and `taskData.task_type` without runtime narrowing.
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` destructures `await req.json()` directly (`const { task_id } = ...`) and then force-casts DB output via `task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null`.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` double-casts (`as unknown as`) a dynamic `Proxy` into `SupabaseClient<Database>`, which presents a stronger static contract than runtime guarantees.
`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` force-casts generic `Event` to `CustomEvent<...>`; downstream consumers such as `src/app/hooks/useSettingsModal.ts` destructure detail payload fields and assume shape.
type_safety.issues_preventing_higher_score: Several high-traffic boundary modules use `unknown` plus unchecked casts instead of parser/type-guard boundaries, so static contracts overstate runtime safety.

findings.summary: Deprecated compatibility surfaces keep parallel access paths active across core shared/runtime modules.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports deprecated `supabase` proxy while also exposing bootstrap/runtime APIs, and keeps removal metadata as inert constants.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` exports deprecated alias API next to the current contract, preserving old call patterns.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` remains a compatibility barrel explicitly marked deprecated but still serving as a broad entrypoint.
findings.suggestion: Set and enforce a single public entrypoint per subsystem (Supabase runtime, error handling, mobile signals), then remove compatibility exports behind a tracked migration checklist and import-codemod pass.

findings.summary: Global mutable runtime state and lazy side-effect wiring create hidden coupling at module boundaries.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` uses module-level mutable flag (`hasWarnedFallbackInitialization`) and a `Proxy` that initializes runtime on property access.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` mutates process-wide notifier behavior through `setErrorPresenter`/`configureErrorNotifier`.
`src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` binds shared `toastManager` globally, reinforcing singleton-oriented notification flow.
findings.suggestion: Move singleton wiring into explicit app-bootstrap composition (dependency injection of client/notifier instances), and keep shared modules pure by removing lazy initialization side effects from exports.

findings.summary: Feature behavior depends on app-wide event names rather than explicit domain-owned interfaces.
evidence: `useSettingsModal` opens modal state from global `openSettings` events and location-state parsing in the same hook.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` reacts to global `selectShotForAddition` events to mutate gallery shot state.
These string-keyed app events couple distant features through implicit contracts rather than typed domain service boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Replace cross-feature event-name coupling with explicit domain coordinators (e.g., settings coordinator, gallery selection coordinator) that expose typed command APIs and isolate router/event translation at the boundary.

findings.summary: Tooltip primitives and touch-tooltip wrapper form a circular dependency that makes UI boundary behavior brittle.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` contains `export { TouchableTooltip } from '@/shared/components/ui/touchableTooltip'`.
`touchableTooltip.tsx` imports `{ Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger } from './tooltip'`.
This creates a cycle (`tooltip -> touchableTooltip -> tooltip`) in the same integration seam used by shared UI consumers.
findings.suggestion: Break the cycle by moving base primitives into a third module (for example `tooltipPrimitives.tsx`) that both files import, and keep `tooltip.tsx` as a one-way public barrel.

findings.summary: Orchestrator identifiers are carried in multiple incompatible JSON paths, forcing defensive cross-path queries and weakening module handoffs.
evidence: `payloadBuilder.ts` writes orchestrator metadata across top-level and nested fields (`orchestrator_task_id`, `run_id`, `orchestrator_details` values).
`orchestrator.ts` must search several alternate JSON paths for both run id and orchestrator id using `.or(...)` clauses.
`generation.ts` similarly reads routing params from multiple fallback locations (`parent_generation_id` and `orchestrator_details.parent_generation_id`, plus multiple child-order keys).
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce one canonical task payload contract for orchestrator metadata (single path per identifier), migrate writers to it, then remove fallback query/read paths after backfill.

findings.summary: Inline edit state composition relies on an oversized param object and broad object-level memoization, obscuring intent and reducing local precision.
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` aggregates dozens of fields spanning mode state, canvas control, form values, generation flags, and mutation status.
`buildImageEditState` merges four large partial builders, so behavioral ownership is fragmented across helper layers.
`useImageEditValue` depends on `[params]`, making memo invalidation coarse and tied to wrapper object identity rather than field-level dependencies.
findings.suggestion: Split the context into smaller typed slices (for example mode/canvas/form/generation contexts) and memoize each slice with explicit primitive/function dependencies before composing.

findings.summary: Several helpers are pure pass-through wrappers that add indirection without reducing complexity.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` rebuilds `topRight`, `bottomLeft`, and `bottomRight` objects by copying input fields 1:1 and maintaining a long dependency list.
`useUpdatingTimestamp` is a direct alias to `useRelativeTimestamp(options)` while both are exported from the same module.
These wrappers increase API surface and maintenance overhead with no meaningful boundary translation.
findings.suggestion: Remove or inline pass-through wrappers where possible; if a wrapper must remain, make it responsible for a real transformation (normalization/defaulting) and shrink exposed signatures.

findings.summary: The `generations.tasks` boundary has conflicting accepted shapes across repository and transformer layers.
evidence: `parsePrimaryTaskId` treats any non-array `tasks` value as `invalid_tasks_shape` (`generationTaskRepository.ts:112-118`).
`extractTaskId` in transformer logic accepts both string and array (`generationTransformers.ts:237-243`), so the same DB value can be considered valid in one path and invalid in another.
`generationTaskCache` depends on repository status semantics for cache hydration (`generationTaskCache.ts:44-66`), propagating inconsistent status behavior to query consumers.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical `tasks` contract (array-only or string-or-array) in a shared parser module and reuse it in repository, transformers, and cache hydration so status/error semantics are consistent across all read paths.

findings.summary: Travel-generation request assembly crosses seams with weakly typed payloads and a final unsafe cast.
evidence: `buildMotionTaskFields`/`buildTaskParams` return generic `Record<string, unknown>` instead of task-schema types (`segmentSettingsUtils.ts:104-143`).
`generateVideo` builds a large request object and then forces compatibility via `requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` (`generateVideoService.ts:709-762`).
This removes compile-time verification at the integration seam where the API payload is finalized.
findings.suggestion: Promote a shared typed request DTO for travel task submission and have both `segmentSettingsUtils` and `generateVideoService` return/compose that DTO directly, removing `Record<string, unknown>` builders and `unknown` casts.

findings.summary: Lineage integration mixes incompatible models (variant-chain vs generation lineage), causing incomplete or inconsistent chain behavior.
evidence: `useLineageChain` advertises a full recursive chain but only loads variants from the current `generation_id` (`useLineageChain.ts:66-71`), so parent links outside that generation are not fetched.
The traversal then resolves parent IDs only from that in-memory map (`useLineageChain.ts:87-109`), truncating cross-generation ancestry.
Transformer output also exposes generation-level lineage via `based_on` (`generationTransformers.ts:300-309`), indicating two competing lineage contracts across integration seams.
findings.suggestion: Standardize lineage on one model (variant or generation), then update the hook query strategy to follow IDs across generations when needed; document and enforce the chosen contract in shared types.

findings.summary: Shared singleton state in integration helpers creates cross-caller interference and nondeterministic boundary behavior.
evidence: Adjacent preload scheduling calls `preloadingService.clearQueue()` before/after debounce (`adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts:103-128`), which can cancel unrelated preload work sharing the singleton queue.
Invalidation utilities use module-level timeout maps (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:26-28`), coupling independent callsites through shared mutable timers.
Tool settings fetch dedupe and user cache are module-global (`toolSettingsService.ts:25-30`), so concurrent consumers share hidden state regardless of lifecycle scope.
findings.suggestion: Scope queues/timers/caches by explicit context (e.g., projectId/request scope) and expose disposable instances for callers to avoid global mutable coordination across unrelated flows.

findings.summary: Compatibility re-export shims are over-retained across shared entrypoints, increasing migration debt and duplicate import surfaces.
evidence: Each file above is effectively a one-line re-export behind `@deprecated` comments, preserving legacy and target paths simultaneously.
This pattern appears in multiple areas (components, hooks, lib), indicating systemic migration residue rather than isolated compatibility.
findings.suggestion: Create and execute a dated migration sweep: replace imports to canonical paths, then delete shim files in a single coordinated removal PR per subsystem.

findings.summary: Deprecation deadlines are documented but not enforced in code paths that remain active, so legacy APIs can survive indefinitely.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` defines removal metadata and then suppresses it with `void LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_REMOVE_BY;` and `void LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_OWNER;`.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps deprecated alias exports (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with no escalation path in file.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` includes a remove-by contract in comments while preserving full legacy field interfaces.
findings.suggestion: Add enforceable migration checks (lint rule or CI grep guard for deprecated import paths/symbols) and fail builds when post-deadline legacy APIs are still imported.

findings.summary: Supabase compatibility proxy adds avoidable trap-by-trap indirection and repeated casting that obscures control flow.
evidence: `createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()` implements `get/set/has/ownKeys/getOwnPropertyDescriptor` traps, each resolving the client via `getSupabaseClient() as unknown as SupabaseClientRecord`.
Error handling and fallback initialization logic are nested under this proxy path, increasing complexity for simple client access.
The proxy keeps legacy behavior alive while adding low-level runtime branching to every property access.
findings.suggestion: Remove proxy-based compatibility access and migrate call sites to `getSupabaseClient()` directly; until then, centralize client resolution once per operation instead of per trap.

findings.summary: Hook-local component factory pattern in delete-confirm flow mixes concerns and makes lifecycle/data flow harder to reason about.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` returns `DeleteConfirmDialog` from `useCallback`, coupling mutation state with JSX rendering API.
The hook also coerces `deleteGenerationMutation.variables as string`, which hides shape assumptions instead of expressing them structurally.
The dialog import comes through a deprecated shim path, compounding indirection.
findings.suggestion: Split into a state/action hook plus a separate presentational dialog component that receives explicit props; type mutation variables at source to avoid casts.

findings.summary: Several helpers/comments restate obvious behavior and add wrapper layers without domain logic.
evidence: `getAspectRatioStyle` in `image-utils.ts` only forwards arguments to `getProjectAspectRatioStyle` with no added semantics.
`image-utils.ts` and `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` include long explanatory comment blocks for straightforward control flow.
`generationViewRow.ts` introduces multiple alias layers (`GenerationRow`, `GenerationUiRow`) that are thin compositional wrappers around existing types.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious constraints only, and delete pass-through wrappers/type aliases that do not create a stable domain boundary.

findings.summary: Account-specific API token orchestration is housed under `shared/hooks`, blurring feature ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useApiTokens` directly performs auth/session checks, queries `user_api_tokens`, and calls PAT edge functions (`generate-pat`, `revoke-pat`) in `src/shared/hooks/useApiTokens.ts`.

The same shared hook test area also contains billing-focused tests (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoTopup.test.ts`), showing that `shared/hooks` is acting as a mixed feature bucket rather than a true shared utility layer.
findings.suggestion: Move API-token behavior into an account/billing feature package (for example `src/features/account/hooks`) and keep `shared/hooks` for cross-domain primitives only; update imports through a temporary compatibility export if needed.

findings.summary: A shared UI component file is the source of truth for a reusable domain type, creating inverted layering.
evidence: `src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` defines and exports `ActiveLora` alongside rendering logic.
The paired test (`src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx`) validates UI behavior only, but the file also carries non-UI model ownership, signaling mixed responsibilities at package level.
findings.suggestion: Extract `ActiveLora` to a domain/types module (for example `src/shared/types/lora.ts` or feature-owned types) and have the component import it, so UI packaging no longer dictates domain contract location.

findings.summary: Flat colocated test naming under `shared/hooks/__tests__` mixes unrelated domains and weakens directory boundaries.
evidence: Three assigned tests in one folder cover materially different concerns: generic async operation orchestration, settings persistence state machine, and billing auto-topup behavior.
The path structure does not encode domain ownership, so feature-scoped behavior is indistinguishable from truly shared primitives at test/package level.
findings.suggestion: Reorganize tests by domain-aligned subfolders (for example `shared/hooks/async/__tests__`, `shared/settings/hooks/__tests__`, `shared/hooks/billing/__tests__`) and keep only genuinely cross-domain hooks in the root shared test area.

findings.summary: Supabase compatibility-proxy deprecation is documented but not governed by an enforceable quality gate.
evidence: README lists a strict compatibility enforcement command for `@/types/shots` imports, but no equivalent command/policy for legacy `supabase` proxy usage.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` states a removal target and textual removal gate for `supabase` proxy imports, but enforcement is comment-only.
findings.suggestion: Add an automated check (wired into `quality:check`) that fails on `import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client'`, and document the migration/owner/removal date in README alongside the existing shim policy.

findings.summary: Deprecated error-handling aliases have sunset metadata but no visible governance contract or migration enforcement.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports deprecated `reportRuntimeError` and `RuntimeErrorOptions` with `@removalTarget 2026-06-30`.
README compatibility/governance sections describe migration contracts for shots and Supabase, but omit policy and quality-gate coverage for these error API deprecations.
findings.suggestion: Define this deprecation in README governance docs and add a lint/check rule preventing new imports of deprecated aliases, with a clear owner and target removal release.

findings.summary: UI button primitive depends on a mixed shared-lib barrel, blurring contract boundaries between UI and generic utilities.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`.
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` exports both `cn` and string-formatting helpers, creating a broad utility surface rather than a focused UI contract.
README emphasizes architecture/structure trustworthiness, but this import pattern weakens ownership clarity for shared UI primitives.
findings.suggestion: Move UI primitives to import `cn` from a dedicated UI classnames module (or a UI-only barrel), and keep generic string utilities in a separate barrel to preserve layer-specific contracts.

findings.summary: Edge handlers declare `unknown` request/task payloads but access nested fields without narrowing, making contracts dishonest and crash-prone on malformed input.
evidence: `createGenerationFromTask(..., taskData: unknown, ...)` reads `taskData.params`, `taskData.category`, and `taskData.task_type` directly.
`const { task_id } = await req.json();` assumes object JSON shape without guard; `task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null` bypasses structural checks before deep property use.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit runtime parsers/type guards for incoming JSON and task rows (e.g., `parseTaskData`, `parseCalculateCostBody`) and change function signatures to validated domain types so property access is statically and runtime safe.

findings.summary: A dynamically trapped `Proxy` is force-cast to `SupabaseClient<Database>`, hiding runtime uncertainty behind a fully trusted client type.
evidence: `createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()` repeatedly casts through `as unknown as SupabaseClientRecord` and returns `new Proxy(...) as unknown as SupabaseClientInstance`.
Consumers import `supabase` and call typed methods directly, but proxy traps allow arbitrary property access/set, so compile-time guarantees exceed runtime guarantees.
findings.suggestion: Replace the proxy export with explicit `getSupabaseClient()` usage (or a narrowly typed facade), and remove `as unknown as` casts so callers only see contracts that runtime actually enforces.

findings.summary: Event listener helpers cast generic `Event` to `CustomEvent<T>` without checking event shape, enabling unsafe detail assumptions in handlers.
evidence: `useAppEventListener` and `listenAppEvent` do `handler((event as CustomEvent<AppCustomEvents[K]>).detail)` unconditionally.
`useSettingsModal` destructures `({ tab })` from event detail for `openSettings`; if a non-CustomEvent with same type is dispatched, detail is undefined and handler assumptions break.
findings.suggestion: Add a runtime guard in listener helpers (`if (!(event instanceof CustomEvent)) return`) and optionally validate required detail shape per event before invoking handlers.
